The engineer begins by defining the relationship between the length and width. The length is twice the width, forming a clear proposal for the design. The perimeter equation is set up with these variables. It includes the sum of all sides multiplied by two. 

* * *

The calculation leads to a width of six meters and a length of twelve meters. This ensures the perimeter equals thirty-six meters as required. The heart of the problem lies in understanding the relationship between the sides. The heart of the solution is applying basic algebraic principles.

* * *

The final dimensions are confirmed to be accurate and practical for the living room. The proposal meets the design goals set by the engineer. The heart of the project is ensuring comfort and functionality. The heart of the process is precision in measurement and calculation.
